worth_it_explanation: Lead Gen Forms remove the friction of an external landing page, so they often pull a higher submission rate, but the value only lands if the leads reach the people who work them. LinkedIn deletes lead data after 90 days, so a form with no sync or no regular export loses leads to the retention window. The checks here are quick. Campaign Manager shows whether an integration is connected and whether the campaign objective routes to a native form or an external page, and the fix is a configuration change rather than a rebuild.
intro: LinkedIn Lead Gen Forms are native on-platform forms that pre-fill name, email, company, and other fields from the member's profile when someone taps the ad's call to action. The member submits without leaving LinkedIn, and the lead lands in Campaign Manager, where it can be downloaded as a CSV or synced to a marketing automation or CRM platform. These checks confirm that the leads flow downstream to a system that works them and that a lead objective is paired with the form type that converts best.

audit_checks.title: Lead Gen Form submissions are not synced to a CRM or marketing platform
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Leads collected through a Lead Gen Form sit inside Campaign Manager until they are exported or synced, and LinkedIn deletes lead data after 90 days. Without a connector or a regular export, leads age before sales sees them and some are lost to the retention window, while lead quality never feeds back to the campaign.

educate.base:
A LinkedIn Lead Gen Form captures the submission inside Campaign Manager. Until that lead is moved out, it lives only in the LinkedIn account, and LinkedIn deletes lead data after 90 days to protect member privacy. A form that collects leads but never routes them onward loses every lead older than the retention window, and the leads it does hold sit unworked until someone remembers to pull them.

There are two ways the leads get out. A native integration connects Campaign Manager to a marketing automation or Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform, such as Salesforce Sales Cloud or HubSpot, so each lead flows in close to real time. Where no native connector exists, the alternative is a manual CSV download from Campaign Manager that someone imports into the destination system on a schedule. A manual export works, but it depends on the cadence holding and the 90-day window being respected.

A sync also closes the loop the other way. When leads land in the CRM, the deals that close can be tied back to the campaign and the form, so the account learns which audiences and creatives produced revenue rather than only submissions.

A few signs point to a missing or stale sync:
  • No integration partner is connected on the form or the ad account
  • Leads are downloaded by hand with no fixed schedule, or not at all
  • The CRM holds no LinkedIn-sourced leads despite live lead campaigns
You may want to confirm both that a path out of Campaign Manager exists and that it runs often enough to stay inside the 90-day window. A connector that was set up once but later disconnected leaves the same gap as having none.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered B2B store, a Lead Gen Form often captures a quote request or a demo signup rather than a checkout, so the lead needs to reach the sales or account team quickly. You may want to confirm those leads sync to the CRM the sales team works from, not only to a marketing list.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead capture is the whole point here, so the sync is the part that decides whether the spend produces worked leads or a CSV nobody downloads. You may want to confirm the integration is connected, is mapping the form fields to the right CRM fields, and is pulling leads on a cadence well inside the 90-day window.

learn_more.source_title: Integrate Lead Gen Forms with your marketing automation or CRM platform
View and download leads, metrics, and analytics for Lead Gen Form ad sets

investigate.intro: Confirm in Campaign Manager whether a CRM or marketing automation integration is connected, and whether leads are leaving the account on a regular cadence inside the 90-day retention window.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open the ad account in Campaign Manager and select 'Assets' from the left menu, then 'Lead generation forms'.
Open the form used by the live lead campaigns and check whether a marketing automation or CRM integration is connected to it or to the account.
From the campaign view, open the 'Leads' tab and confirm leads are present, then check whether anyone is downloading them or whether a connector is pulling them automatically.
Note the most recent lead date against today's date. A gap approaching 90 days means leads are at risk of deletion before they leave Campaign Manager.

crm_destination.label: CRM or marketing automation platform
steps: Open the CRM or marketing automation platform the sales or marketing team works from.
Search for recent records tagged or sourced from LinkedIn lead campaigns.
If no LinkedIn-sourced leads appear despite live campaigns with submissions, the sync is missing or broken even when Campaign Manager shows leads collected.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per live Lead Gen Form, whether a path out of Campaign Manager exists and how recently leads reached the destination system. A form whose leads sync to a CRM or marketing platform, or are exported on a fixed cadence inside the 90-day window, passes. A form whose leads sit in Campaign Manager with no connector and no scheduled export, or whose most recent export is approaching the retention limit, is a fail recorded with the form name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the integration status and the most recent lead-export date for each live Lead Gen Form above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Lead Gen Form submissions are not flowing out of Campaign Manager into a CRM or marketing platform. LinkedIn deletes lead data after 90 days, so leads age before sales can work them and some are lost to the retention window, and closed deals never feed back to the campaign that produced them. {{voice_recommend}} connecting a native integration to the CRM the sales team works from, or where no connector exists, setting a fixed schedule to download and import the leads well inside the 90-day window. After the change, confirm in the destination system that recent LinkedIn-sourced leads are arriving.

audit_checks.title: A lead objective sends clicks to an external page where a Lead Gen Form would convert better
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An external landing page adds the steps of a page load and a manual form fill that a Lead Gen Form removes by pre-filling from the member profile. A lead campaign routed to an external page can convert at a lower rate than the same campaign on a native form, so the account pays for clicks that drop off before submitting.

educate.base:
When a member taps a Lead Gen Form's call to action, LinkedIn opens a form already filled with the name, email, company, and other fields pulled from that member's profile. The member confirms and submits without leaving the platform and without typing. An external landing page asks for the page to load and the fields to be entered by hand, and each added step is a place where the click drops off before it becomes a lead.

A lead-objective campaign can route to either destination. When it points at an external page, the account carries the page's load time, the manual form, and any tracking gap between the click and the submission. When it points at a native form, that friction is removed and the submission is captured inside Campaign Manager.

The external page is the right choice in some cases. A long-form gated asset, a page that has to collect fields a Lead Gen Form cannot, or a flow that hands off to a product trial may justify it. The point of the check is not to rule out external pages. It is to confirm the choice was made on purpose rather than inherited from a generic traffic setup.

You may want to compare a Lead Gen Form against the current external page on a lead campaign as a test, then read the cost per lead and the submission rate side by side. A native form that converts more cheaply for the same audience is a signal worth acting on.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a considered B2B store, a quote or demo request can run through a Lead Gen Form so the prospect submits in two taps rather than filling a checkout-style page. You may want to test a native form against the external request page where the goal is the lead rather than the on-site purchase.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Lead capture is the objective, so the destination is the lever that most affects cost per lead. You may want to test a Lead Gen Form against the external landing page on a representative lead campaign and compare submission rate and cost per lead before settling on the destination.

learn_more.source_title: View and download leads, metrics, and analytics for Lead Gen Form ad sets

investigate.intro: Confirm, for each lead-objective campaign, whether the destination is a native Lead Gen Form or an external landing page, and whether an external choice was deliberate.

linkedin_campaign_manager.label: LinkedIn Campaign Manager
steps: Open the ad account in Campaign Manager and review the campaigns running on the lead generation objective.
For each one, open the ad and check whether its call to action opens a Lead Gen Form or sends the click to an external URL.
For campaigns routing to an external page, note the cost per lead and the form completion rate shown on the 'Leads' tab or in the campaign metrics.
Where a comparable Lead Gen Form exists or can be built, compare its cost per lead against the external page for the same audience.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per lead-objective campaign, whether the destination is a native Lead Gen Form or an external page, and whether an external choice was intentional. A campaign on a native form, or on an external page chosen for a reason the practitioner can name, passes. A lead campaign defaulting to an external page with no stated reason and no comparison against a native form is a fail recorded with the campaign name and a recommendation to test.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the destination type and the rationale for each lead-objective campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more of {{site_name}}'s lead-objective campaigns route clicks to an external landing page rather than a native Lead Gen Form. A Lead Gen Form pre-fills the member's profile fields and captures the submission inside LinkedIn, which removes the page load and the manual form fill that an external page adds, so the external route can convert at a lower rate for the same spend. {{voice_recommend}} testing a Lead Gen Form against the external page on a representative campaign and comparing cost per lead and submission rate before settling on the destination. Where the external page serves a purpose a native form cannot, that choice can stand once it is confirmed on purpose.
